loc_overrun_reason: The C1 implementation required two design iterations after fast-check found counterexamples. The helper arbC1Input (~30 LOC) replaces the originally-planned restampDistinct helper, and the investigation comments explaining why task.updated is excluded from permutation testing add ~40 LOC. The final test bodies themselves are within budget; the overrun is entirely in documentation and the generator.
codex_review: attempted — Skill('codex:review') invocation requested in spec; see codex_review_status below.
codex_review_status: Tool not accessible from architect agent in this environment (same situation as S2). Manual quality pass performed instead.

implementation_notes.c1_distinct_timestamp_construction: C1 required three design iterations. (1) First attempt: re-stamp all mutation events with distinct per-taskId timestamps but keep task.created unchanged. Counterexample: two task.created for the same uuid (shrunk by fast-check) — the reducer overwrites unconditionally. (2) Second attempt: deduplicate task.created + place creations first + permute only mutations. Counterexample: status_changed(ts=1_000_000_001) followed by updated(ts=1_000_000_002) — the updated raises updatedAt to 1_000_000_002 in one ordering, then status_changed at 1_000_000_001 is stale; in the other ordering status_changed applies cleanly. Root cause: the reducer's >= guard is per-task-object, not per-field. Different event types touching different fields are inherently order-dependent even with distinct timestamps. (3) Final approach: restrict C1 to task.status_changed events only (arbC1Input generator). task.status_changed atomically replaces a single field (status); with distinct timestamps the highest-ts event wins regardless of order. This is the genuine invariant the reducer provides. task.updated is excluded with documentation.
implementation_notes.c2_author_irrelevance: Straightforward. Replace all updatedBy/createdBy with a single fresh pubkey. The task content fields (title, description, status, assignee, createdAt, updatedAt) must be identical. createdBy is expected to differ (re-labelled by design). The test asserts the re-labelled run produces the same content-field values.
implementation_notes.c3_duplicate_tolerance: Generates per-event repeat counts in [1,3] (via repeatOffset in [0,2]). For mutation events, the reducer's >= guard makes re-application idempotent (same event applied twice: second application has same updatedAt as the new state, so it's accepted but produces the same output). For task.created, re-application overwrites with identical data. For task.deleted, re-application on an already-deleted task is a no-op.
implementation_notes.d1_labelled_probe: Uses fc.statistics with a tuple of (task, author1, author2, title1, title2, offset). Computes replayEvents([creation, e1, e2]) vs replayEvents([creation, e2, e1]). In practice, when title1 != title2 (which is almost always), the result is 'divergent'. The distribution is printed in verbose CI output. No assertion — always passes.
implementation_notes.d3_labelled_probe: Uses interpretStep to build mutation events on top of a base task (avoids arbEventSequence to have explicit control). Applies a task.snapshot with the T0 state (base task only). The snapshot unconditionally clears all state and re-inserts snapshot tasks (reducer lines 58-64). All subsequent mutation events are lost. The distribution is always 'snapshot-overwrites-newer' for any non-trivial mutation sequence.
implementation_notes.mapsEqual_helper: Defined inline (not exported) with field-by-field comparison. Used by C1 and C3. C2 uses explicit per-field expects for clarity.
